Inside the document card, allocate about 25–30% of the width to the left-hand section navigation and 70–75% to the right-hand document viewer. The loader and status indicator overlay or sit within the viewer area without increasing total allocated space.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain a moderately spacious feel. Tabs are compact but clearly separated with comfortable horizontal padding and subtle gaps. The document card has ample internal padding (16–24px on all sides) and generous line-height for readable legal text. Vertical spacing between the tabs and the document card is around 24px, and between major sections inside the document around 24–32px, echoing the mockup’s breathing room while accommodating text-heavy content.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Follow the mockup’s vertical-first then horizontal visual flow. The eye moves from the top navigation (site-wide), down to the central strip of three horizontally aligned tabs (privacy, terms, cookies), scanning left-to-right to understand the available document types. From the active tab, the eye drops straight down into the main white document card where attention flows horizontally from the left-hand section navigation to the right-hand document text. The status indicator sits near the top-right of the card, subtly in peripheral view, while loading states temporarily draw attention to the center of the document area via an overlay.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s full-width dark navigation bar, the Legal & Privacy page reveals a wide, centered band of content framed by deep charcoal margins on both sides, preserving the workshop-like, artisan atmosphere. At the top of this band, three horizontally aligned tab segments sit side by side, each occupying roughly one-third of the content width, evoking the three-card product row from the mockup. These tabs are slightly elevated against the dark, textured background, with the active tab highlighted in white with a gold accent edge, while inactive tabs are darker and more subdued. Beneath this row, a large, white, card-like panel appears to slightly overlap the darker background above, echoing the overlapping text panel on the product images. This main card stretches across about 70–75% of the viewport width and is centered, with soft shadows and rounded corners that suggest a tangible document resting on a workbench. Inside this card, the left quarter of the space forms a vertical navigation rail listing document sections, while the right three-quarters display the actual legal text in a clean, column-like format. At the top-right of the card, a compact status indicator quietly shows loading and last-updated information, ensuring the content feels precise and up-to-date without introducing clutter. When documents are loading, a semi-transparent dark veil lightly dims the text area and a centered spinner appears, creating a focused, workshop-like moment of transition. Overall, the layout feels calm, structured, and tactile—modern yet craft-oriented—faithfully translating the mockup’s card-row aesthetic into a text-centric legal page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The document-type-router is a non-visual controller positioned conceptually at the top of the legal content band, just beneath the global site navigation and above the visual tabs. It occupies no visible height or width in the layout; instead, it sits in the background, aligning logically with the horizontal strip where the legal-document-tabs are rendered. As the user selects different tabs or arrives via a deep-linked URL, this router updates the active document type and orchestrates which document content is shown in the main white card below. In a mental picture of layers, the router sits on an invisible layer behind the tabs and viewer, spanning the same central 70–80% width as the visible legal content, but it does not alter spacing, margins, or any visual composition.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Background controller occupying no visual space; logically associated with the tabs and viewer, but not rendered in the UI.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-document-loader is another non-visual component that manifests only when actively loading data. When a new document type is selected, it triggers an overlay confined strictly to the main document card area (the white panel below the tabs). This overlay stretches from the card’s inner left padding edge to its inner right padding edge and from just below the card’s header row (title and status area) down to the bottom padding, preserving the card’s rounded corners and edges. The overlay is a semi-transparent dark veil that subtly dims the underlying text, with a centered loading indicator placed both horizontally and vertically within the visible area of the document content (roughly at the midpoint of the right-hand content column). This ensures the loader feels integrated into the content space rather than a full-screen interruption, matching the contained, workshop-like aesthetic. When not loading, the loader collapses and occupies no visible space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as a centered overlay within the bounds of the document viewer area; otherwise non-rendered. Does not shift underlying layout, only floats above it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-document-tabs are positioned at the top of the legal content band, directly below the site-wide navigation bar and centered within the page. They span approximately 70–80% of the viewport width, aligned to the same max-width grid as the rest of the content, with generous dark side margins framing them. The tabs are arranged in a single horizontal row of three equal segments (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Cookies Policy), each roughly one-third of the available width and sharing a consistent height of about 56–64px. This arrangement echoes the mockup’s three-card row, but in a more compact, control-like form. Each tab is rendered as a pill or card-like rectangle with 16–24px horizontal padding inside, and a comfortable 8–12px vertical padding around its label. The active tab appears as a white or light panel with a subtle drop shadow and a thin gold highlight along its top or left edge, visually ‘popping’ forward from the dark background. Inactive tabs are darker (deep grey) with lighter text, separated from one another by narrow gaps of about 8px, which preserves the sense of three distinct cards. Vertically, there is about 24px of space below the tab row before the main document card begins, allowing the tabs to feel like a distinct selection strip rather than merging into the content panel beneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, centered horizontal control anchored to the top of the main legal content area; it scrolls away with the page content and does not stick to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-document-viewer is the primary white card-like panel positioned directly beneath the tabs, centered in the viewport. It spans the same central 70–75% of the viewport width as the tabs, aligning to the implicit 12-column grid (e.g., occupying roughly 8–10 central columns with 1–2 columns of margin on each side). Vertically, its top edge sits about 24px below the bottom of the tab row. To echo the mockup’s overlapping text panel, the viewer card appears to slightly intrude into the darker background space above: its top edge can be visually aligned so that it feels like it is sliding up under the tabs, with a soft shadow to emphasize depth. The card has generous internal padding of 24px on all sides. Inside this card, the top region (about 72–96px high) is reserved for a document title and the status indicator, spanning across the full width. Below this header area, the card’s interior is split into two columns: a left navigation column occupying about 25–30% of the card’s width, and a right content column for the legal text occupying about 70–75%. The right content region is where the legal text flows in a single scrollable column, with line-height and 16–20px font size for readability, and section headings spaced with 24–32px vertical margins for clarity. On longer pages, the entire card scrolls as part of the page, but the overall impression remains that the user is reading a single, large, well-crafted document laid out on a workbench-like surface. On narrow viewports, the card can collapse to a single-column layout, stacking the section navigation above the text, while still preserving the central alignment and generous padding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static central content card that scrolls with the page. Internally, the right text area scrolls normally; the card itself remains a single, unified visual block.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-document-status-indicator sits inside the main document card, aligned to the top-right corner of the card’s header region. Horizontally, it is right-aligned with the inner padding edge of the card (about 24px from the card’s right border), and vertically it is positioned roughly 16px below the card’s top padding edge, placing it in the same band as the document title, which is aligned to the left. The indicator itself is compact, occupying no more than 20–25% of the card’s width and a height similar to a single line of text plus 4–8px padding. It can appear as a subtle pill-shaped label or a small horizontal strip with a muted background (slightly darker than the white card, or a soft grey) and gold or white text showing status messages such as “Loading…”, “Last updated: [date]”, or “Draft / Effective”. When loading states are active, a small spinner icon may appear to the left of the text inside this indicator. The placement ensures it is immediately discoverable when looking at the top of the document, yet unobtrusive while reading; it does not introduce extra vertical spacing beyond the existing header band. As the content scrolls, the status indicator moves with the card, remaining at the top of the content until scrolled off-screen, matching the page’s natural flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within the document card header, right-aligned; scrolls away naturally with the document content and does not stick to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-document-section-navigation occupies the left-hand column inside the main white document card, forming a vertical navigation rail for the currently selected document. It begins just below the card’s header region (beneath the title and status indicator), and extends down to the card’s bottom padding. Horizontally, it uses about 25–30% of the card’s width, starting at the card’s left inner padding edge (about 24px from the card’s outer border). The section navigation is rendered as a vertical list of section titles (e.g., “1. Introduction”, “2. Data We Collect”, etc.), each separated by 8–12px of vertical spacing, with slightly increased spacing (16–20px) between logical groups. The active section is highlighted with either a gold accent bar on the left edge of the item or a light background fill, creating a strong link to the rustic gold accents of the overall theme. To reflect the workshop aesthetic and the mockup’s card spacing, the navigation list is contained within the white card but may have a subtle divider line along its right edge, visually separating it from the main text. On taller documents, this navigation column can be scrollable within its own area or use a sticky behavior: the list remains fixed within the left column while the right content scrolls. At medium and large screen sizes, this rail remains visible alongside the content to support quick intra-document jumps; on narrow viewports, it can collapse into a horizontal or collapsible menu placed above the content, still respecting the overall central alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inside the document card, occupying the left column. On larger screens, its contents may use a sticky behavior within the card’s scroll context, staying visible as the user scrolls the document text; on smaller screens it stacks above the text and scrolls normally.
